en: The words of the Preacher, the son of David, king in Jerusalem.
zh: 以下是傳道者，就是在耶路撒冷作王、大衛的兒子所說的話。

en: Vanity of vanities, says the Preacher, vanity of vanities! All is vanity.
zh: 傳道者說：虛空的虛空。 虛空的虛空，一切都是虛空。

en: What does man gain by all the toil at which he toils under the sun?
zh: 人的一切勞碌，就是他在日光之下的勞碌， 對自己有甚麼益處呢？

en: A generation goes, and a generation comes, but the earth remains forever.
zh: 一代過去，一代又來， 地卻永遠存在。

en: The sun rises, and the sun goes down, and hastens to the place where it rises.
zh: 太陽升起，太陽落下， 匆忙回到它上升之處。

en: The wind blows to the south and goes around to the north; around and around goes the wind, and on its circuits the wind returns.
zh: 風向南颳，又往北轉， 循環周行，旋轉不息。

en: All streams run to the sea, but the sea is not full; to the place where the streams flow, there they flow again.
zh: 江河向海裡流，海卻不滿溢； 江河之水歸回本源，循環流轉。

en: All things are full of weariness; a man cannot utter it; the eye is not satisfied with seeing, nor the ear filled with hearing.
zh: 萬事都令人厭倦， 人說，說不盡； 眼看，看不飽； 耳聽，聽不足。

en: What has been is what will be, and what has been done is what will be done, and there is nothing new under the sun.
zh: 已有的事必再有， 作過的事必再作； 日光之下並無新事。

en: Is there a thing of which it is said, "See, this is new"? It has been already in the ages before us.
zh: 有甚麼事人可以說： “看啊！這是新的”？ 其實，很久之前已經有了， 在我們之前早就有了。

en: There is no remembrance of former things, nor will there be any remembrance of later things yet to be among those who come after.
zh: 以前的事，無人記念； 將來的事，後來的人也不追憶。

en: I the Preacher have been king over Israel in Jerusalem.
zh: 那時，我傳道者在耶路撒冷作以色列的王。

en: And I applied my heart to seek and to search out by wisdom all that is done under heaven. It is an unhappy business that God has given to the children of man to be busy with.
zh: 我曾用智慧專心尋求查究天下所發生的一切事；原來 神給予世人的，是勞苦的擔子，叫他們為此煩惱。

en: I have seen everything that is done under the sun, and behold, all is vanity and a striving after wind.
zh: 我看過日光之下所發生的一切事，不料，一切都是虛空，都是捕風。

en: What is crooked cannot be made straight, and what is lacking cannot be counted.
zh: 彎曲的，不能弄直；缺少的，不能數算。

en: I said in my heart, "I have acquired great wisdom, surpassing all who were over Jerusalem before me, and my heart has had great experience of wisdom and knowledge."
zh: 我自己心裡說：“我得了極大的智慧，勝過所有在我以前統治耶路撒冷的人；我見識了許多智慧與知識。”

en: And I applied my heart to know wisdom and to know madness and folly. I perceived that this also is but a striving after wind.
zh: 我又專心究察智慧和知識，狂妄和愚昧，才知道這也是捕風。

en: For in much wisdom is much vexation, and he who increases knowledge increases sorrow.
zh: 因為多有智慧，就多有煩惱；加增知識，就加增痛苦。
